commonIssues: Root and stem rot from overwatering or poorly drained soil — the most common killer; always let soil dry between waterings
Mealybugs (remove with rubbing-alcohol-soaked cotton swab); also watch for spider mites, scale, and aphids
Leaf drop, spotting, and dwarfing from extreme drought — despite being a succulent it still needs regular water in the growing season
Leggy, drooping, deep-green growth when light is insufficient (needs 4+ hours of direct sun)
Mature plants become top-heavy and tip over — use a heavy pot; insecticide sprays can scorch succulent leaves
Freezing temperatures kill the plant; bring indoors before first frost (tolerates only the lightest frost)

source.citation: Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC, 'Jade Plant' factsheet — needs four or more hours/day of direct sun (south window); keep soil moist in spring/summer, dry between waterings in winter; ideal 18-24 C day, 10-13 C night; fertilize every 3-4 months; cactus mix or soil/peat/coarse sand; mealybugs, spider mites, root rot.
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC, 'Jade Plant' — daytime 65-75 F (18-24 C), night 50-55 F (10-13 C); liquid houseplant fertilizer every 3-4 months.

University of Wisconsin-Madison Extension Horticulture, 'Jade Plant, Crassula ovata' — four or more hours of direct sun; water liberally in active growth letting soil dry between deep waterings, keep mostly dry in winter; fertilize every two months during active growth; high-porosity succulent/cactus mix; survives light frost but freezing kills it; grows well in restricted container root space; mealybugs and spider mites.

University of Wisconsin-Madison Extension Horticulture, 'Jade Plant, Crassula ovata' — well-drained high-porosity succulent/cacti potting mix with extra perlite or sharp sand; repot every 2-3 years; tolerates being root-bound for years.

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder, Crassula ovata — USDA zones 11-12; allow soils to dry between waterings (indoors typically every 2-3 weeks); intolerant of moist poorly drained soils; bright light with some afternoon shade; well-drained loamy potting mix; appreciates reduced-watering rest from fall to late winter; aphids, scale, spider mites, mealybugs.

RHS Plant Profile, Crassula ovata (jade plant) — hardiness H2 (minimum 1-5 C, does not survive freezing); full sun, sheltered, south/west-facing; bushy succulent evergreen sub-shrub to 1.5-2.5 m; water to keep lightly moist in growth, very sparingly in winter dormancy; general liquid fertilizer monthly spring-summer; pots of cactus compost.

The Old Farmer's Almanac, 'Jade Plant Care Guide' — at least 6 hours of bright light daily (young plants indirect, established plants tolerate direct sun); room temperature 18-24 C, cooler at night/winter down to 13 C; let soil dry thoroughly between waterings; very susceptible to rot.

Confidence note: container volumes are estimates — no extension source gives pot liters; 2 L (~15 cm pot) minimum chosen because UW-Madison Extension notes jade grows well in restricted container root space and tolerates being root-bound, with heavier/larger pots (up to ~10 L) for mature top-heavy specimens per Clemson HGIC.

Confidence note: baseIntervalDays 14 is a conservative midpoint of the 'every 2-3 weeks indoors, soil dry between waterings' guidance (Missouri Botanical Garden / extension consensus); hotWeatherMultiplier 0.75 and heatStressC 32 are estimates from the general guidance to water more liberally in active warm-season growth and to shade from intense afternoon sun.

Confidence note: seasonality months derive from Missouri Botanical Garden's 'resting period with reduced watering from fall to late winter' and RHS/extension spring-summer active-growth feeding guidance, mapped to Northern Hemisphere months.
